association with underlying genetic disorders rather than age or typical childhood infections like herpangina which remains more prevalent despite vaccination programs against related diseases such as Agent1 concludes that smallpox is now incredibly rare because it has been completely eliminated thanks to successful global vaccination campaigns led by organizations like the WHO. Hence, choosing "smallpox" aligns perfectly with what would be expected medically nowadays. 

**Summary:** Herpangina and dental cysts occur frequently in children, whereas cherubs mainly affects individuals genetically predisposed; therefore, smallpox stands uniquely rare owing to widespread eradication achieved through effective vaccines distributed internationally per directives set forth by bodies like the WHO. **Thus,** smallpox fits the description of something extraordinarily unusual happening infrequently if ever anymore amidst contemporary healthcare scenarios focused heavily preventing communicable illnesses entirely eliminating threats altogether wherever possible.
